title: Introduction to msaVariant
text: msaVariant overlays clinical-genetics evidence onto multiple sequence alignment (MSA) single ggplot2 figure. layer paints different kind evidence: geom_variant() — patient’s variant(s) geom_domain() — InterPro/Pfam protein domains geom_gnomad() — gnomAD allele frequencies geom_clinvar() — ClinVar pathogenicity calls geom_alphamissense() — AlphaMissense silico scores geom_revel() / geom_cadd() — REVEL / CADD scores geom_track() — generic per-residue track (bring data)

title: Introduction to msaVariant
text: five annotation-database layers (geom_gnomad, geom_clinvar, geom_alphamissense, geom_revel, geom_cadd) geom_domain fetch data Zenodo deposit first time use gene, cache locally next time. package code ships clinical-genetics data — visualisation machinery. means: package install small fast. always get data version pinned package release, figure reproducible. Updating data (gnomAD releases v5, example) matter running build scripts re-uploading Zenodo. first time call e.g. geom_gnomad(gene = "PATL1", msa = fa), package download ~100 KB Zenodo cache tools::R_user_dir("msaVariant", "cache"). Subsequent calls instant.

title: Introduction to msaVariant
text: ’s . Five layers, one gene symbol per annotation, manual data download.
code: library(ggmsa) library(msaVariant)  # 1. Your alignment: PATL1 orthologs around residue 518 fa <- system.file("extdata", "patl1_orthologs.fasta",                   package = "msaVariant")  # 2. The patient's variant patient <- data.frame(   pos         = 29,                # K518 in the demo stretch   pos_end     = 61,                # frameshift through end of window   label       = "K518fs",   consequence = factor("frameshift",                        levels = c("frameshift","missense","nonsense")) )  # 3. The figure ggmsa(fa, char_width = 0.6, seq_name = TRUE) +   geom_variant(patient,                  msa = fa) +   geom_domain(gene = "PATL1",            msa = fa) +   geom_clinvar(gene = "PATL1",           msa = fa) +   geom_alphamissense(gene = "PATL1",     msa = fa) +   geom_gnomad(gene = "PATL1",            msa = fa)

title: Introduction to msaVariant
text: already custom annotation (lab-internal database, paper’s supplementary table, etc.), pass via data = instead gene =: data = gene = modes mutually exclusive — pass one , never .
code: my_annotations <- read.csv("my_annotations.csv") ggmsa(fa) +   geom_track(my_annotations,              msa = fa, value = "score",              name = "My score")

title: Introduction to msaVariant
code: cache_location()       # where the cache lives clear_cache()          # nuke everything clear_cache("gnomad")  # only the gnomAD slice

title: Introduction to msaVariant
text: use msaVariant published work, please cite package underlying data sources (see Zenodo deposit README full citation list).

title: Tutorial: From raw protein sequences to an annotated MSA figure
text: complete workflow: Obtain protein orthologs UniProt (unaligned). Run multiple sequence alignment R. Visualise msaVariant, including patient’s variant database-derived annotation tracks. use PATL1 human gene seven orthologs running example, ’s case msaVariant originally written . workflow generalises single-protein investigation.

title: Tutorial: From raw protein sequences to an annotated MSA figure
text: msaVariant perform alignment . Choose mainstream protein-MSA tool; recommend msa (Bioconductor, ships MUSCLE/ClustalW/ClustalOmega) stays inside R: Standalone alternatives (run shell, load Biostrings::readAAMultipleAlignment): mafft — apt-get install mafft / brew install mafft muscle — apt-get install muscle clustalo — apt-get install clustalo tutorial use msa::msa() MUSCLE.
code: if (!require("BiocManager")) install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("msa")

what: Step 2 — Get the unaligned ortholog sequences
title: Tutorial: From raw protein sequences to an annotated MSA figure
text: need FASTA file protein sequences gene interest human plus several orthologs spanning evolutionary distance want argue . faster reliable route pull canonical sequences UniProt accession: Tip: already FASTA file collaborators, skip download just readAAStringSet("yourfile.fasta").
code: library(Biostrings)  # UniProt accessions for PATL1 across eight species accessions <- c(   "PATL1_HUMAN" = "Q86TB9",        # Homo sapiens   "PATL1_PANTR" = "A0A2I3RIF4",    # Pan troglodytes   "PATL1_MOUSE" = "Q3TC46",        # Mus musculus   "PATL1_RAT"   = "B5DF93",        # Rattus norvegicus   "PATL1_XENLA" = "Q32N92",        # Xenopus laevis   "PATL1_DANRE" = "A2RRV3",        # Danio rerio   "PATR1_DROME" = "Q9VEN9",        # Drosophila melanogaster   "PATR1_CAEEL" = "Q20374"         # Caenorhabditis elegans )  # Pull each FASTA from UniProt. Each download is ~1 KB. seqs <- lapply(accessions, function(acc) {   url <- sprintf("https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/%s.fasta", acc)   s   <- readAAStringSet(url)   s[1]   # first (canonical) sequence in the file })  # Rename to readable IDs and concatenate unaligned <- do.call(c, seqs) names(unaligned) <- names(accessions)  # Save the unaligned FASTA in case you want to inspect it writeXStringSet(unaligned, "patl1_unaligned.fasta")

title: Tutorial: From raw protein sequences to an annotated MSA figure
text: see multiple alignment length 800-1300 columns (varies alignment software’s gap insertions).
code: library(msa)  aln <- msa(unaligned, method = "Muscle", type = "protein") aln  # Convert to a plain FASTA file that `msaVariant` can read aa_aligned <- as(aln, "AAMultipleAlignment") writeXStringSet(as(aa_aligned, "AAStringSet"),                 filepath = "patl1_aligned.fasta")

what: Step 4 — Pick a residue window of interest
title: Tutorial: From raw protein sequences to an annotated MSA figure
text: full alignment wide render single figure. Pick residue range human reference variant sits. PATL1 K518fs case, focus residues 490..550: Extract window smaller FASTA plotting:
code: library(msaVariant)  # Use the package's coord-map utility to convert # "human residue 490..550" into "alignment columns N..M" map <- build_msa_coord_map("patl1_aligned.fasta",                             ref_name = "PATL1_HUMAN") start_col <- map$msa_col[match(490, map$residue_pos)] end_col   <- map$msa_col[match(550, map$residue_pos)] c(start_col, end_col) aa <- readAAStringSet("patl1_aligned.fasta") window <- subseq(aa, start = start_col, end = end_col) writeXStringSet(window, "patl1_window.fasta")

title: Tutorial: From raw protein sequences to an annotated MSA figure
text: pos column window-local residue numbering (1 = first residue window). package translates alignment columns automatically.
code: patient <- data.frame(   pos         = 518 - 490 + 1,    # position within the window   pos_end     = 550 - 490 + 1,    # frameshift extends to end of window   label       = "K518fs",   consequence = factor("frameshift",                        levels = c("frameshift","missense","nonsense")),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )

title: Tutorial: From raw protein sequences to an annotated MSA figure
text: first time call geom_*(gene = ...), package downloads per-gene annotation slice Zenodo deposit (~50–200 KB ) caches . Subsequent runs instant.
code: library(ggmsa)  ggmsa("patl1_window.fasta",       char_width = 0.6, seq_name = TRUE) +   geom_variant(patient,                    msa = "patl1_window.fasta") +   geom_domain(gene = "PATL1",              msa = "patl1_window.fasta") +   geom_clinvar(gene = "PATL1",             msa = "patl1_window.fasta") +   geom_alphamissense(gene = "PATL1",       msa = "patl1_window.fasta") +   geom_gnomad(gene = "PATL1",              msa = "patl1_window.fasta")

title: Tutorial: From raw protein sequences to an annotated MSA figure
text: “Residue position X found” — variant outside window extracted. Either widen window check residue numbering (full-protein vs window-local). Sequence names don’t match — geom_* defaults first sequence FASTA reference. human PATL1 isn’t first, supply ref_name = "PATL1_HUMAN" every layer. Empty annotation track — geom_gnomad(gene = "FOO") returned NULL. Either gene isn’t Zenodo deposit, internet/firewall blocked download. Try clear_cache(); get_gnomad("FOO") see actual error. figure dominated gnomAD/CADD/AlphaMissense tracks — take ~1 row vertical space. 8 sequences + 5 annotation tracks, set fig.height = 7 larger.

title: Tutorial: From raw protein sequences to an annotated MSA figure
text: gene, changes : UniProt accessions Step 2. gene symbol geom_*(gene = "...") calls. residue window Steps 4-5. Everything else stays .

text: Name Y (2026). msaVariant: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation Variant Overlay. R package version 0.2.0, https://github.com/ramizkrdnz/msaVariant.
code: @Manual{,   title = {msaVariant: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation with Variant Overlay},   author = {Your Name},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://github.com/ramizkrdnz/msaVariant}, }

what: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation with Variant Overlay
title: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation with Variant Overlay
text: msaVariant produces publication-quality figures single genetic variant: cross-species protein multiple sequence alignment per-column conservation, stacked annotation tracks (ClinVar, gnomAD, AlphaMissense, REVEL, CADD, protein domains), automatic ACMG evidence-code strip (PS1, PM1, PM2, PM5, PP3) computed straight data. built top ggmsa returns normal ggplot/patchwork object, every layer, colour, label stays fully customisable figures can drop paper.

title: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation with Variant Overlay
text: Install visual base ggmsa (Bioconductor), msaVariant GitHub:
code: # ggmsa (required base) + Bioconductor deps if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))   install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("ggmsa")  # msaVariant (development version) # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("ramizkrdnz/msaVariant")

title: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation with Variant Overlay
text: One call assembles whole figure. example runs box — uses small synthetic DEMO1 bundle ships package (via import_local_bundle()), Zenodo download external data needed:  ⚠️ DEMO1 synthetic. Every number figure fabricated illustrative data — real AlphaMissense / CADD / REVEL / ClinVar / gnomAD predictions. exists let example run ---box show figure layout. DEMO1 real gene. synthetic variant DEMO1 p.R21H engineered five ACMG codes fire — PS1, PM1, PM2, PM5, PP3 — computed automatically bundled tables, exactly real data.
code: library(msaVariant)  # 1. Load the bundled synthetic DEMO1 example into the cache (no Zenodo) import_local_bundle(   system.file("extdata", "DEMO1.rds", package = "msaVariant"),   gene = "DEMO1" )  # 2. Build the full overlay figure for the demo variant DEMO1 p.R21H plot_variant_overlay(   gene          = "DEMO1",   aligned_fasta = system.file("extdata", "demo_aligned.fasta",                               package = "msaVariant"),   variant_pos   = 21,   variant_label = "p.R21H" )

title: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation with Variant Overlay
text: Full articles documentation site, https://ramizkrdnz.github.io/msaVariant/: Get Started — install, load data, build first overlay. Full workflow tutorial — end--end, FASTA finished figure. ACMG Evidence Codes — PS1/PM1/PM2/PM5/PP3 computed (preparation). Colour Schemes — journal, colorblind (Okabe–Ito), grayscale, per-element overrides (preparation). Annotation Tracks — ClinVar / gnomAD / AlphaMissense / REVEL / CADD domain geoms (preparation). Toggling Layers — show/hide track; layout re-flows gaps (preparation). Bring Data — overlay annotations geom_track() (preparation).

title: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation with Variant Overlay
text: Zenodo bundle required — overlay per-residue values already :
code: my_data <- data.frame(pos = 510:530, score = runif(21)) ggmsa::ggmsa(fa) +   geom_track(my_data, msa = fa, value = "score", name = "Custom track")

title: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation with Variant Overlay
text: Zenodo data deposit yet published, remote fetches (fetch_gene_data() gene-symbol annotation layers) currently fail. deposit live, use data via Bring---data mode , load local bundle import_local_bundle() (Quick Example). note removed deposit uploaded wired .

title: Clinical-Genetics Multiple Sequence Alignment Visualisation with Variant Overlay
text: msaVariant released Artistic-2.0 license. Note data fetched via geom_alphamissense() licensed CC--NC-SA 4.0, restricts commercial use works derived . Check license every annotation source display reusing figure.

code: clear_cache(gene = NULL)

text: Invisibly, number files deleted.

what: Compute triggered ACMG evidence codes for one variant — compute_acmg_codes
title: Compute triggered ACMG evidence codes for one variant — compute_acmg_codes
text: Evaluates fixed subset ACMG/AMP evidence codes single missense substitution using annotation tables carried gene bundle. Returns just codes triggered.

title: Compute triggered ACMG evidence codes for one variant — compute_acmg_codes
code: compute_acmg_codes(bundle, variant_pos, aa_change, pp3_min_predictors = 2L)

title: Compute triggered ACMG evidence codes for one variant — compute_acmg_codes
text: bundle gene bundle: named list elements domains, clinvar, gnomad, alphamissense, revel, cadd (produced import_local_bundle / fetch_gene_data). variant_pos Integer residue position (canonical UniProt numbering). aa_change Character variant identifier, e.g. "R175H" "p.R175H". Ref/alt parsed string. pp3_min_predictors Integer 1-3 (default 2). PP3 fires least many three PP3 predictors (AlphaMissense class == "likely_pathogenic", REVEL > 0.7, CADD phred >= 25) pass threshold.

title: Compute triggered ACMG evidence codes for one variant — compute_acmg_codes
text: character vector triggered codes, canonical evidence-strength order (PS1, PM1, PM2, PM5, PP3); character(0) none fire.

title: Compute triggered ACMG evidence codes for one variant — compute_acmg_codes
text: Rules (computed bundle; external lookups): PS1 ClinVar contains exact aa_change     significance "Pathogenic". PM1 variant_pos falls within start-end range     least one domains row. PM2 aa_change absent gnomAD,     af_joint 0.0001. PM5 ClinVar record position different     alt residue significance "Pathogenic"     "Likely_pathogenic". PP3 Count many three predictors pass — AlphaMissense     am_class == "likely_pathogenic", REVEL revel_score > 0.7,     CADD cadd_phred >= 25; triggered count least     pp3_min_predictors.

title: Compute triggered ACMG evidence codes for one variant — compute_acmg_codes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ b <- fetch_gene_data("TP53") compute_acmg_codes(b, 175, "R175H") } # }

what: Per-column conservation score for an MSA — conservation_score
title: Per-column conservation score for an MSA — conservation_score
text: Per-column conservation score MSA

title: Per-column conservation score for an MSA — conservation_score
code: conservation_score(   msa,   method = c("shannon", "js"),   background = NULL,   ignore_gaps = TRUE )

title: Per-column conservation score for an MSA — conservation_score
text: msa MSA form accepted `build_msa_coord_map()`. method One `"shannon"` (default) `"js"` (Jensen-Shannon divergence background). background Optional named vector background residue frequencies. `NULL` `method = "js"`, uniform background used. ignore_gaps `TRUE` (default), gap characters excluded per-column frequency calculation.

title: Per-column conservation score for an MSA — conservation_score
text: data.frame columns `msa_col` `score`.   Shannon entropy score nats, rescaled 1 = fully   conserved 0 = uniform across observed alphabet.   Jensen-Shannon, score [0, 1] 1 = fully conserved.

what: Construct an empty per-gene object conforming to the spec — empty_gene_data
title: Construct an empty per-gene object conforming to the spec — empty_gene_data

title: Construct an empty per-gene object conforming to the spec — empty_gene_data
code: empty_gene_data(   gene = "UNKNOWN",   uniprot_id = NA_character_,   protein_length = 0L )

title: Construct an empty per-gene object conforming to the spec — empty_gene_data

what: Fetch the combined annotation file for a gene — fetch_gene_data
title: Fetch the combined annotation file for a gene — fetch_gene_data
text: Downloads (reads local cache) per-gene annotation bundle Zenodo deposit. Returns deserialized list described `DATA_FORMAT_SPEC.md`.

title: Fetch the combined annotation file for a gene — fetch_gene_data
code: fetch_gene_data(   gene,   force_refresh = FALSE,   validate = TRUE,   verify_checksum = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Fetch the combined annotation file for a gene — fetch_gene_data
text: gene HGNC gene symbol (e.g. `"PATL1"`). force_refresh `TRUE`, redownload even cached. validate `TRUE` (default), validate file package's format spec returning. verify_checksum `TRUE` (default), local `MANIFEST.tsv` present, verify bundle's sha256 manifest entry; mismatched file treated corrupt, removed cache, `NULL` returned. effect manifest present. quiet `TRUE`, suppress "Downloading..." messages.

title: Fetch the combined annotation file for a gene — fetch_gene_data
text: named list 7 elements (`meta`, `domains`,   `clinvar`, `gnomad`, `alphamissense`, `revel`, `cadd`),   `NULL` warning failure.

title: Fetch the combined annotation file for a gene — fetch_gene_data
text: users call directly; `get_domains()`, `get_clinvar()`, etc. helpers `geom_*()` layers route transparently.

title: Overlay AlphaMissense per-residue mean scores onto an MSA — geom_alphamissense

title: Overlay AlphaMissense per-residue mean scores onto an MSA — geom_alphamissense
text: data Optional pre-built data.frame; see `DATA_FORMAT_SPEC.md` schema. `NULL`, supply `gene`. gene HGNC symbol; triggers fetch Zenodo deposit. msa, ref_name Reference MSA sequence name. summary aggregate 19 alt scores position. One `"mean"` (default), `"max"`, `"median"`. y_offset, track_height Track geometry.

title: Overlay AlphaMissense per-residue mean scores onto an MSA — geom_alphamissense
text: list ggplot2 layers (`NULL`).

code: geom_track(   data,   msa,   ref_name = NULL,   value,   type = c("continuous", "discrete"),   name = "",   palette = NULL,   value_range = NULL,   y_offset = -2,   track_height = 1.2 )

title: Overlay an arbitrary per-residue annotation track onto an MSA — geom_track
text: data data.frame least: * `pos`   — integer residue position (1-based reference) * value column whose name pass via `value` msa, ref_name MSA reference sequence name (parent `ggmsa()` call). value Name column `data` holding per-residue value plot. type Either `"continuous"` (heatmap strip, default) `"discrete"` (categorical tick row). name Label printed right track. palette `type = "continuous"`, vector colours passed `colorRampPalette()`. `type = "discrete"`, named vector mapping levels `value` colours. value_range continuous tracks, length-2 numeric vector giving `c(min, max)` colour scale. Defaults observed range. y_offset, track_height Vertical placement height MSA-row units.

title: Overlay an arbitrary per-residue annotation track onto an MSA — geom_track
text: list ggplot2 layers.

title: Overlay an arbitrary per-residue annotation track onto an MSA — geom_track
text: generic, bring---data layer annotation tracks. Use gnomAD allele frequencies, ClinVar significance, AlphaMissense / REVEL / CADD scores, post-translational-modification sites, ChIP-seq signal, anything else can map residue.

title: Overlay an arbitrary per-residue annotation track onto an MSA — geom_track
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Continuous: gnomAD allele frequencies from your own query my_af <- data.frame(pos = 510:530, af = runif(21, 0, 1e-3)) geom_track(my_af, msa = fa, value = "af",            name = "gnomAD AF", type = "continuous")  # Discrete: ClinVar calls from your own export my_cv <- data.frame(pos = c(498, 518),                     sig = c("Benign", "Pathogenic")) geom_track(my_cv, msa = fa, value = "sig", type = "discrete",            name = "ClinVar",            palette = c(Benign = "#4575B4", Pathogenic = "#D7301F")) } # }

what: Overlay variants onto a multiple sequence alignment — geom_variant
title: Overlay variants onto a multiple sequence alignment — geom_variant
text: Adds vertical bands marking alignment columns affected one clinical / population variants. Works tidy data frame; VCF input use `read_vcf_variants()` first.

title: Overlay variants onto a multiple sequence alignment — geom_variant
code: geom_variant(   variants,   msa,   ref_name = NULL,   colour_by = c("consequence", "pathogenicity"),   alpha = 0.5,   show_label = TRUE,   label_y = NULL,   ... )

title: Overlay variants onto a multiple sequence alignment — geom_variant
text: variants data.frame minimum column `pos` (integer, 1-based protein residue position reference). Optional columns: * `pos_end`   : integer, indels/frameshifts; defaults                 `pos` (single-residue variant) * `label`     : character, shown band * `consequence` : factor, used colour band                   (e.g. "missense", "frameshift", "nonsense",                   "synonymous", "splice") * `pathogenicity` : factor, alternative colour key (e.g.                   "Pathogenic", "Likely_pathogenic", "VUS",                   "Likely_benign", "Benign") msa, ref_name MSA used parent `ggmsa()` call, reference sequence within . geom maps variant positions alignment columns via `map_variant_to_msa()`. colour_by Either `"consequence"` (default) `"pathogenicity"`, controlling column drives fill. alpha Band transparency `[0, 1]`. Default `0.5`. show_label Logical; whether draw labels band. label_y Numeric vertical position variant label. Defaults `n_seq + 3` (just MSA, clear default `geom_domain()` track `n_seq + 1.7`). ... Passed underlying `geom_rect()` layer.

title: Overlay variants onto a multiple sequence alignment — geom_variant
text: ggplot2 layer (list layers) can `+`-ed   onto `ggmsa()` plot.

title: Overlay variants onto a multiple sequence alignment — geom_variant
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggmsa) fa <- system.file("extdata", "patl1_orthologs.fasta",                   package = "msaVariant") v  <- data.frame(pos = 518, pos_end = 577,                  label = "p.K518fs",                  consequence = "frameshift") ggmsa(fa, start = 500, end = 580) +   geom_variant(v, msa = fa, ref_name = "PATL1_HUMAN") } # }

what: Import a locally-built gene bundle into the cache — import_local_bundle

code: import_local_bundle(   path,   gene = NULL,   overwrite = TRUE,   verify_checksum = TRUE )

title: Import a locally-built gene bundle into the cache — import_local_bundle
text: path Path `.rds` file produced build script. file must conform gene-data schema enforced [validate_gene_data()]. gene Optional gene symbol; `NULL` (default) symbol read `bundle$meta$gene` inside file. Used destination filename: `<GENE>.rds`. overwrite Logical, default `TRUE`. `FALSE` bundle gene already present, error thrown. verify_checksum Logical, default `TRUE`. local `MANIFEST.tsv` present lists gene, imported file's sha256 compared manifest entry warning emitted mismatch. file still imported (asked explicitly); warning simply flags possible corruption stale manifest. effect manifest present.

title: Import a locally-built gene bundle into the cache — import_local_bundle
text: Invisibly, destination path imported bundle.

title: Import a locally-built gene bundle into the cache — import_local_bundle

what: msaVariant: Clinical-genetics MSA visualisation with variant overlay — msaVariant-package
title: msaVariant: Clinical-genetics MSA visualisation with variant overlay — msaVariant-package
text: Extends `ggmsa` annotation layers tailored clinical rare-disease genetics workflows.

title: msaVariant: Clinical-genetics MSA visualisation with variant overlay — msaVariant-package
text: package provides (v0.1): * `geom_variant()`        — user variants (tidy data frame) * `geom_domain()`         — protein domain track (user-supplied) * `geom_track()`          — generic per-residue annotation track                             user-supplied data (gnomAD AF,                             ClinVar significance, AlphaMissense                             scores, PTM sites, etc.) * `conservation_score()`  — per-column Shannon / Jensen-Shannon                             computed MSA Plus utility functions: * `build_msa_coord_map()` — residue--column lookup * `map_variant_to_msa()`  — vectorised position mapping * `scale_fill_pathogenicity()` — colourblind-safe ACMG palette package bundle: Population-genetics pathogenicity databases (gnomAD, ClinVar, AlphaMissense, REVEL, CADD) change frequently, licensed ways constrain redistribution, large ship static files. Users supply annotation data via `geom_track()`. future v0.2 add optional online fetchers pull databases demand cache results locally.

title: msaVariant: Clinical-genetics MSA visualisation with variant overlay — msaVariant-package
text: Maintainer: Name @example.org Authors: Name @example.org contributors: Guangchuang Yu (ggmsa, package builds) [contributor]

what: Generate a publication-grade variant-overlay figure — plot_variant_overlay
title: Generate a publication-grade variant-overlay figure — plot_variant_overlay
text: Composes multi-panel figure (variant header, MSA, annotation tracks, legend dashboard) clinical variant context evolutionary conservation population/clinical annotations. gene's annotation data read package cache via [fetch_gene_data()]. user supplies gene symbol, aligned FASTA, variant position.

title: Generate a publication-grade variant-overlay figure — plot_variant_overlay
code: plot_variant_overlay(   gene,   aligned_fasta,   variant_pos,   variant_label = NULL,   window = NULL,   ref_name = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   aa_change = NULL,   pp3_min_predictors = 2L,   color_scheme = "journal",   acmg_colors = NULL,   variant_highlight_color = NULL,   track_palettes = NULL,   msa_color_scheme = NULL,   show_acmg = TRUE,   show_domain = TRUE,   show_clinvar = TRUE,   show_gnomad = TRUE,   show_cadd = TRUE,   show_alphamissense = TRUE,   show_revel = TRUE,   acmg_codes = c("PS1", "PM1", "PM2", "PM5", "PP3") )

title: Generate a publication-grade variant-overlay figure — plot_variant_overlay
text: gene Gene symbol (string). Must match name `.rds` bundle cache. Use [import_local_bundle()] add local bundle, [fetch_gene_data()] download Zenodo. aligned_fasta Path aligned FASTA file (e.g. MUSCLE output). reference sequence (matching canonical UniProt isoform) one sequences file. variant_pos Residue position reference (UniProt canonical numbering). Can also string like `"R175H"` `"p.R175H"` position parsed. variant_label Optional display label (e.g. `"p.R175H"`). `NULL`, derived `variant_pos` reference residue. window Length-2 integer vector `c(start, end)` defining residue range display. Defaults ±20 residues around `variant_pos`, clipped protein. ref_name Name reference sequence FASTA. `NULL`, sequence whose name contains gene symbol used; otherwise first sequence. title Plot title. subtitle Plot subtitle. aa_change Optional variant identifier used ACMG evidence strip, e.g. `"R175H"`. `NULL`, parsed `variant_label` carries full ref+pos+alt substitution. ACMG codes require alternate allele shown complete `aa_change` available. pp3_min_predictors Integer 1–3 (default 2) forwarded [compute_acmg_codes()]: PP3 fires least many three PP3 predictors (AlphaMissense/REVEL/CADD) pass threshold. color_scheme Figure color preset: `"journal"` (default, publication-quality, colorblind-safe, grayscale-legible, muted), `"colorblind"` (explicit Okabe-Ito), `"grayscale"` (pure black--white print). acmg_colors Optional named vector overriding ACMG chip colors (names among `PS1`,`PM1`,`PM2`,`PM5`,`PP3`); partial allowed. `NULL` uses scheme. variant_highlight_color Optional single color variant highlight fill; `NULL` uses scheme. track_palettes Optional named list overriding individual track palettes: `clinvar`, `gnomad`, `cadd`, `alphamissense`, `revel`. `NULL` uses scheme. msa_color_scheme Optional MSA coloring: scheme name (`"journal"`, `"colorblind"`, `"clustal"`, `"grayscale"`) explicit named per-residue color vector. `NULL` uses scheme. show_acmg, show_domain, show_clinvar, show_gnomad, show_cadd, show_alphamissense, show_revel Logical toggles (`TRUE` default) annotation layer. Setting one `FALSE` fully removes layer figure re-flows (patchwork heights row assignments adjust number active layers — blank gaps). variant header MSA always shown. acmg_codes Character vector ACMG codes *display* (default five: `c("PS1","PM1","PM2","PM5","PP3")`). full ACMG computation always runs internally; codes set actually triggered drawn chips.

title: Generate a publication-grade variant-overlay figure — plot_variant_overlay
text: `patchwork` plot object. Save `ggplot2::ggsave()`.

title: Generate a publication-grade variant-overlay figure — plot_variant_overlay
code: ## Runnable with the synthetic DEMO1 example bundle shipped in the ## package (fabricated data; not real predictions). Use a temporary ## cache so the example does not touch your real cache directory. Sys.setenv(MSAVARIANT_CACHE = tempfile("msaVariant_cache_")) import_local_bundle(   system.file("extdata", "DEMO1.rds", package = "msaVariant"),   gene = "DEMO1" ) #> Cache directory does not exist yet (no annotations have been downloaded). #> Imported DEMO1 bundle -> /tmp/RtmpB0BDjI/msaVariant_cache_1a583882f1df/0.1.0/DEMO1.rds p <- plot_variant_overlay(   gene          = "DEMO1",   aligned_fasta = system.file("extdata", "demo_aligned.fasta",                               package = "msaVariant"),   variant_pos   = 21,   variant_label = "p.R21H" )  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Save a publication-resolution figure ggplot2::ggsave("demo1_R21H.png", p,                 width = 15, height = 9, dpi = 300, bg = "white") } # }

title: Resolve the full color specification for a variant-overlay figure — resolve_plot_colors
text: color_scheme One `"journal"` (default), `"colorblind"`, `"grayscale"`. acmg_colors Optional named character vector overriding ACMG chip colors (names among PS1/PM1/PM2/PM5/PP3). Partial allowed. variant_highlight_color Optional single color variant highlight fill. track_palettes Optional named list `clinvar`, `gnomad`, `cadd`, `alphamissense`, `revel`. msa_color_scheme Optional MSA palette: scheme name (`"journal"`, `"colorblind"`, `"clustal"`, `"grayscale"`) named per-residue color vector.

title: Resolve the full color specification for a variant-overlay figure — resolve_plot_colors

what: Validate a per-gene annotation object against the package spec — validate_gene_data
title: Validate a per-gene annotation object against the package spec — validate_gene_data
text: Checks object conforms structure defined `DATA_FORMAT_SPEC.md`: presence 7 named elements, required columns, correct types, `NA` required columns. Returns list `valid` (logical) `message` (character) describing issues found.

title: Validate a per-gene annotation object against the package spec — validate_gene_data
code: validate_gene_data(obj, strict = FALSE)

title: Validate a per-gene annotation object against the package spec — validate_gene_data
text: obj deserialized object per-gene `.rds` file. strict `TRUE`, throw error validation failure instead returning list. Default `FALSE`.

title: Validate a per-gene annotation object against the package spec — validate_gene_data
text: `strict = FALSE`: list elements `valid`   (logical) `message` (character vector issues, NULL   valid). `strict = TRUE`: invisibly `TRUE` success,   `stop()` formatted message failure.

title: Validate a per-gene annotation object against the package spec — validate_gene_data
text: Used internally `fetch_gene_data()` every cache hit download. Also useful lab's build pipeline validate files uploading Zenodo.
